steps.title: Recall the margin of error principle
steps.content: The margin of error formula shows that margin of error is inversely proportional to $\sqrt{n}$ — larger sample size leads to smaller margin of error.

steps.title: Calculate the effect of doubling the sample size
steps.content: The sample size increases from 142 to 284 (exactly doubles). Since margin of error is inversely proportional to $\sqrt{n}$, doubling $n$ reduces the margin of error by a factor of $\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \approx 0.707$. The new margin of error is approximately $5.15\% \times 0.707 \approx 3.64\%$, which is lower. That matches choice A — A is correct.
